items.title: Meta, CoreWeave Shares Rise After Expanding $21 Billion AI Cloud Deal
items.summary: Both Meta and CoreWeave logged stock gains Thursday after expanding their existing AI deal, now valued at $21 billion.

items.title: Google's PaperOrchestra AI Converts Lab Notes Into Publication-Ready Research Papers
items.summary: The framework automates the complex process of transforming raw research materials into polished academic manuscripts.

items.title: Another Solo Bitcoin Miner Hits the Jackpot, Scoring $225K BTC Reward
items.summary: An individual Bitcoin miner hit the lottery overnight, scoring a $225,000 BTC reward when they found a block.

items.title: Securitize names ex-SEC official Brett Redfearn as president ahead of public listing
items.summary: Brett Redfearn joins as president as the firm works to build regulated infrastructure for tokenized assets.

items.title: Potential buyers are circling Winklevoss-backed crypto exchange Gemini
items.summary: Interested parties are considering buying select parts of the business, particularly its shuttered European operations, to secure regulatory licenses, rather than pursuing a full takeover.

items.title: U.S. Treasury to loop in crypto sector on hacker warnings shared with traditional firms
items.summary: The Department of the Treasury announced it's letting crypto firms sign up for timely information-sharing on cybersecurity threats.

items.title: TON blockchain claims sub-second finality with Catchain 2.0 upgrade
items.summary: Transactions on the Open Network layer-1 blockchain protocol previously took about 10 seconds to settle before the Catchain 2.0 consensus upgrade.

items.title: Is Iran Collecting Hormuz Tolls in Bitcoin? This Crypto Intelligence Firm Is Skeptical
items.summary: TRM’s Ari Redbord expressed skepticism that Iran and affiliated actors are accepting Bitcoin for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Three reasons why Ether traders expect ETH to hold above $1.8K
items.summary: Onchain and technical data hint that $1,800 may have been the macro price bottom for Ether. Is there sufficient bullish momentum for a rally to $3,000?

items.title: Binance rolls out prediction markets for app using Predict.fun
items.summary: The crypto exchange is mounting a challenge to platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, offering ”gasless” trades as it seeks a share of the $20 billion prediction market.

items.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent Slams Crypto Industry 'Nihilists' as Clarity Act Remains in Limbo
items.summary: Several obstacles to the Clarity Act, which have remained unsolved for months, stand in the way of its passage before a looming deadline.

items.title: MarketVector and Coinbase launch index tracking Bitcoin and tokenized gold
items.summary: A new Bitcoin–gold index highlights shifting views on crypto’s role as a store of value as correlations with equities increase and gold outperforms.

items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu reportedly has signaled a willingness to negotiate with Lebanon.

items.title: Tom Lee's BitMine Uplisted to NYSE as Ethereum Firm Expands Buyback Program to $4 Billion
items.summary: Ethereum treasury giant BitMine Immersion Technologies upped its share buyback program to $4 billion and was uplisted to the NYSE on Thursday.

items.title: OpenAI Pauses UK AI Tech Team-Up With Nvidia Over Energy Costs, Regulation
items.summary: OpenAI's planned Stargate project with Nvidia and Nscale aimed to deploy thousands of GPUs to support AI workloads in Britain.

items.title: Fartcoin's price crashed 50% after $145 million manipulation bet went wrong
items.summary: The liquidation was so large relative to the order book that Hyperliquid's auto-deleveraging mechanism activated, forcibly closing profitable short positions on the other side of the trade to prevent the system from accumulating bad debt.
